common.footerBlurb: AI consulting, across every industry. Built out of MIT.

positionRows.label: We don't
positionRows.body: We don't pick your vendors, run the integration, write your prompts, or build the model. Plenty of firms already do that well.

positionRows.label: We do
positionRows.body: We sit with your leadership team, help them decide which workflows to push on and which to drop, and find out whether people are really using what you've already bought. You get that back in writing every month.

positionRows.label: Why it works
positionRows.body: Once the people side is sorted out — who owns each workflow, who's accountable for the change, who acts on what the tools produce — the technology decisions tend to get a lot easier.

home.failuresIntro: We've sat through a lot of stalled pilots, across retail, software, telecom, and healthcare. The software almost always worked fine. A few of the same things went wrong instead.
home.closerSub: A 30-minute call with the partners. No pitch deck.

failures.heading: The decision happens too far from the work
failures.body: Leadership signs the contract and the team gets told to use it. Nobody asked the people doing the job what was actually slowing them down, so the work stays the same and only the login screen changes.
failures.symptom: Most people quit using it within a month.

failures.heading: Nobody actually owns it
failures.body: IT owns the software and operations owns the process, but the handoff between them belongs to no one. The tool produces a recommendation, it lands in a queue, and there's no one whose job is to do something about it.
failures.symptom: Plenty of activity, no change in the numbers.

failures.heading: No one is really measuring it
failures.body: Everyone assumes people are using it. Six months later, no one can say who's on the tool, for which tasks, or whether it actually helped — so the contract gets renewed on a hunch.
failures.symptom: The renewal is basically a guess.

home.heroLede: Most AI rollouts don't stall because the technology is bad. They stall because nobody planned for how people would actually use it. That's the part we help you get right — before, during, and after the tools go in.

engagement.heroTitle: Four ways we work with you.
engagement.heroLede: We're not the firm that installs the software. We work on everything around it: a steady monthly relationship with your leadership, outside experts when you need them, events to learn from other companies, and help hiring the people who'll run it.

lines.v: Two leadership sessions a month, plus a survey that tracks how your team is really using AI.
Calls with AI builders and startup operators when you want a fresh take, not the usual playbook.
Workshops that bring together leaders from companies that don't compete, usually in Cambridge.
Students from MIT, Harvard, and Stanford, placed into roles on your team.

engagement.retainerH2: Two sides of the same work.
engagement.retainerLede: Every month we work both ends: the leaders making the calls, and the people actually using the tools day to day. We meet with your steering committee and survey the wider team, and the picture gets clearer the longer we do it.

surfaces.p: A standing pair of monthly sessions with your AI steering committee — usually the CEO, the COO or head of ops, and the product or function lead.
list: Which workflows to work on, drop, or leave alone
How last month's rollout actually landed with the team

surfaces.p: A short survey that goes to the people actually using the tools each day. It tracks what they're using, what for, and where it's helping.
list: Which AI tools they use, and on which task
Where the tools save time, and where they cost it
What they have stopped using, and why
One signal we carry into the next SteerCo

engagement.cadenceLede: About three hours of our time and one hour of your leadership's, every month.

cadenceItems.p: One hour with the person who owns your AI program. We pick what we're looking at this month.
The monthly survey goes to the team. Five minutes, anonymous, broken out by group.
One hour. We walk the program owner through what the survey turned up and draft the write-up.
One hour with the steering committee. Recommendations, decisions, and what we look at next.

engagement.surveyLede: It takes about five minutes, it's anonymous, and it goes to the people doing the work rather than the executives. The answers get more useful the longer we run it.

surveyQuestions.p: Which AI tools did you use this month, and on which task?
Estimate the hours this tool saved you, on which workflow.
Where did the tool cost you time, or break a working process?
What did you stop using this month, and what made you stop?
What would you most like us to look at next?

memoRows.v: A few things the team flagged this month, and anything they've stopped using.
The workflow we worked on last month, whether the team actually took to it, and whether to keep it, drop it, or fix what's around it.
One thing to work on, one to drop, and one question for the CEO. Signed by the partner on your account.
What the committee agreed in the room, and who owns each decision. We carry it into next month's write-up.

engagement.priceBlurb: Month-to-month. No setup fee. Cancel at the end of any month, in writing.
pricingIncluded: Two monthly working sessions with the AI program owner
One monthly SteerCo hour with the leadership team
Monthly employee AI-use survey, fielded and analyzed
A signed, written SteerCo memo every month
pricingAddon: Expert calls at $450/hr (Line 02), priority event seats (Line 03), and talent search (Line 04) — all open to retainer clients.
pricingNot: We don't pick vendors, do the integration work, or build the model. You're not paying us for any of that.
engagement.enEyebrow: A fresh take, when you want one
engagement.enH2: Not the expert network you already pay for.
engagement.enLede: Networks like GLG connect you with people who've already run the playbook you know. We do something different. We put you in touch with AI builders and younger startup operators doing things your industry hasn't tried yet — so you hear a new approach, not a version of your own.
enThem: How they did it at a big incumbent
Tends to confirm what you already do
Priced for the occasional deep dive
enUs: How they'd build it from scratch today
More likely to challenge your approach
Priced so you can use it often
engagement.enRateP: Retainer clients only. No minimum, no commitment, no markup beyond the expert's rate.
enHowto: You name the question in your SteerCo memo.
We match a builder from the network within 48 hours.
Billed monthly, on top of the retainer.

enDomains.what: How they'd build your workflow with no legacy system to work around.
Whether the model behind your tool is current or a couple of years behind.
How a small team ships in weeks what a big company would scope for a year.
What the workflow could feel like if you started from the user, not the org chart.
Channels and tactics that haven't reached your category yet.
What it really takes to set things up, from people who've done it on a budget.

engagement.eventsH2: Getting the right people in a room.
engagement.eventsLede: A few times a year we bring together leaders from companies that don't compete — usually in Cambridge, sometimes on the road — to work through what AI is actually changing about how they run things. Working sessions, not keynotes.

eventsCards.p: Hands-on sessions on one topic — setting up tracking for a workflow, reading an adoption survey, deciding what to drop. Run by the partners, kept small.
Small standing groups of CEOs and AI sponsors from non-competing companies who meet over a season to compare notes and not feel like they're figuring it out alone.
We're based in Cambridge, close to the labs and the people we came up with. If a group would rather meet somewhere else, we'll come to you.

engagement.eventsNote: Retainer clients get priority seats and member pricing. Standalone seats are available to qualifying companies.
engagement.placementH2: Hiring the people who'll run it.
engagement.placementLede: We're based in two of the best places in the world to find AI talent. We help place graduate and undergraduate students into roles on your team — people who grew up with these tools and can bring that to your company.

placementGeo.p: Our home. Engineers, applied scientists, and early-career operators out of the labs and the business schools.
Our west-coast pipeline. Product builders and ML talent from Stanford and the surrounding startup ecosystem.

engagement.placementNote: Placement runs as a search, scoped to the role. Available to retainer clients first.
